'''Kira I''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Kira system/Legends|Kira system]], a part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Kira sector/Legends|Kira sector]]. It was an uninhabitable world with no apparent resources of value. Kira I was [[Scout/Legends|surveyed]] by [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] scouts visiting the Kira [[Star system/Legends|system]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[70 BBY/Legends|70 BBY]]. At some point following the initial survey, Kira I and [[Kira II|two]] [[Kira III|other]] planets of the system were [[Mundicide|destroyed]] by a cataclysm of unknown origin, leaving behind an [[Kira Prime|asteroid field]].

==Description==
Kira I was an uninhabitable [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Kira system/Legends|Kira system]]. Occupying the closest [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around its [[Kira's sun|sun]],<ref name="TSoK" /> the planet was a part of the [[Kira sector/Legends|Kira sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> which lay in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
The area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] surrounding the Kira [[Star system/Legends|system]] was [[Scout/Legends|explored]] between [[8000 BBY]] and [[5500 BBY/Legends|5500 BBY]]. Between [[1004 BBY]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], near the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|end]] of the [[New Sith Wars]], the system was situated in territory considered either unaligned or contested by the warring [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]].<ref name="TEA" />

[[File:Kira_System.png|thumb|right|250px|The Kira system, site of Kira I until the latter's destruction]]
[[Standard year/Legends|Years]] before<ref name="TSoK" /> [[70 BBY/Legends|70 BBY]],<ref name="Date">[[Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)|''Twin Stars of Kira'']] establishes that the initial [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] survey of the [[Kira system/Legends|Kira system]] took place [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the discovery of the [[Kira Run]]. {{HoloNetNewsWeb|52||Today in History}} dates the discovery of that [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] to forty-eight years before the date [[22 BBY/Legends|13:4:18]], which translates to [[70 BBY/Legends|70 BBY]], according to reasoning presented [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> a Republic scouting team visited the system, giving it only a cursory [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] scan. The scouts did not detect any evidence of valuable resources on Kira I.<ref name="TSoK" />

When a follow-up survey of the Kira system was conveyed years later, it was discovered that the [[Kira II|three]] [[Kira III|inner]] planets of the system, including Kira I, had been [[Mundicide|destroyed]] by a cataclysm of unknown origin, with only a [[Kira Prime|massive asteroid field]] remaining in their place. No cause for the planets' destruction could be determined by the survey, and further exploration of the Kira system was abandoned.<ref name="TSoK" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kira I was first mentioned in [[Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)|''Twin Stars of Kira'']], a [[1993]] supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Within the book, the planet was mentioned in a section authored by [[Stephen Crane]].<ref name="TSoK" />

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Kira system, and therefore Kira I, in grid square N-15.<ref name="TEA" /> However, the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to ''The Essential Atlas'' later changed the placement to grid square N-16, which this article assumes to be correct.<ref name="EAOC" />
